prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Atom‐condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰, and dual descriptor for ferrocene; most-electrophilic site (maximum f⁺).
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical self-consistent tight-binding) as implemented in the xtb backend.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (ferrocene.xyz, 21 atoms); no re-optimization – single-point evaluations for the neutral (N), cationic (N-1), and anionic (N+1) states (notation: GFN2-xTB//user geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent specified).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation +1 doublet; anion −1 doublet.
Software: chemkit calling xtb 6.x (GFN2-xTB Hamiltonian).
Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity status "ok" with trustworthy true. The engine emitted three advisory warnings (verbatim below) noting charge-conservation checks and header presence.
Live log & files: calculation streamed to a live .out log (path printed at launch); result JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpbturq0n5/live.json, bar-chart PNG at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_o3/20260704-190834_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.

Result
Most electrophilic atom: Fe (atom 11, 0-based index 10) with f⁺ = 0.22885456 (dimensionless).
Most nucleophilic atom: Fe (atom 11) with f⁻ = 0.25938191.

Verbatim engine warnings
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

The calculation therefore identifies the iron center as both the preferred electrophilic and nucleophilic site within ferrocene at the GFN2-xTB level in the gas phase.
